hops.context: In a simple trust model the Root-CA acts as the trust anchor of the tree
In a simple trust model the Root-CA acts as the trust anchor of the tree

question: In a simple trust model, how does the relationship between Root Certificate Authorities (Root-CAs) and entity classification function?

options.A: Each Root-CA independently determines its entity classification without any cross-certifications affecting the outcome.
options.B: Root-CAs cross-certify each other and one acts as a trust anchor, directly determining the entity's classification.
options.C: Root-CAs are cross-certified by an external entity that sets the classification for all entities in the model.
options.D: Root-CAs do not interact with each other, and each one classifies entities based on its internal policies.

explanation: In a simple trust model, Root-CAs cross-certify each other to enhance mutual trust and one acts as a trust anchor, directly influencing or determining the classification of the entity in the model. This forms a trust tree where the anchor plays a central role in entity classification.
context: Cross certification (roots certify each other) | In a simple trust model the Root-CA acts as the trust anchor of the tree | In a simple trust model the Root-CA acts as the trust anchor of the tree
